cards.name: Friends Coffee & Tea
cards.address: 1029 S Bascom Ave, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Friends Coffee & Tea with a solid cup and something light to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone and this place is worth the stop.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the water — it's easy, shaded, and actually pleasant on a cool morning like this.
cards.why: Perfect temperature for a trail walk before the day heats up.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art and spend time in both the galleries and the sculpture garden out back.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely good contemporary art that most people sleep on.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market has a dozen vendors under one roof — grab tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good, and snag a patio seat.
cards.why: Easiest way to eat well downtown without committing to one thing.

cards.name: Beat The Lock Escape Room
cards.address: 2131 The Alameda A, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at Beat The Lock Escape Room and actually work for your fun for an hour.
cards.why: A great way to spend a weekday afternoon when you want something more hands-on.

cards.name: Oasis Thai Spa
cards.address: 2203 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the active part of the day with a Thai massage at Oasis Thai Spa in Campbell before heading to the evening.
cards.why: After a walk and an escape room, your body will thank you.

cards.name: 10th Annual Queer Film Festival: Will & Harper
cards.blurb: Catch Will & Harper at the 10th Annual Queer Film Festival tonight in Santa Clara. It's free and a genuinely great film worth seeing on a big screen.
cards.why: Free film festival screening on a Tuesday night is a pretty solid deal.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a stroll along the creek trail. Easy, flat path that kids can handle without complaints, and the shaded sections feel great on a cool morning.
cards.why: Perfect way to shake off the morning before the day gets busy.

cards.name: Puffin Donuts
cards.address: 1442 Pollard Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Puffin Donuts for a late-morning treat. Grab a box of fresh donuts and hot drinks for the grown-ups while the kids pick their favorites.
cards.why: Kids and donuts is never a bad combo, and this place is the real deal.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for the late morning stretch. Walk the lakeside trail, let the kids run the open grass, and if the little train is running they'll absolutely lose it.
cards.why: This is the classic family park in the area and the cool weather makes it even more enjoyable.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for an early dinner. Grab tacos from one vendor, ramen from another, let everyone pick what they want and meet at a patio table.
cards.why: Something for everyone at one stop, which is exactly what you need at the end of a full day with kids.

cards.name: Courage Anyone Live Music Series: OG Factor
cards.address: 399 S 1st St Floor 2, San Jose
cards.blurb: OG Factor plays the Courage Anyone Live Music Series just up the street. Free show, casual vibe, and the kids will get a kick out of live music while you wind down.
cards.why: Free live music a short walk from dinner is a great low-key ending to the day.

cards.name: Tico Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 1334 Dell Ave Suite G, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a coffee at Tico Coffee Roasters. It's a solid local roaster with good beans and a chill vibe to ease into the day.
cards.why: Every good Tuesday starts with a proper cup.

cards.name: Development Review Committee
cards.blurb: Sit in on the Development Review Committee meeting and see what's actually being planned for Los Gatos. It's genuinely interesting to hear what changes are coming to the neighborhood.
cards.why: Free, local, and way more engaging than you'd expect.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail and shake off the morning. It's shaded, flat, and runs right along the creek, so it's an easy stretch without feeling like a workout.
cards.why: 52 degrees in the morning turns into perfect walking weather by midday.

cards.name: El Guapo's
cards.address: 266 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to El Guapo's for lunch. Order something off the board and grab a seat. It's quick, cheap, and hits the spot.
cards.why: You can't go wrong at under $15 when the food is this good.

cards.name: Drop-In Tech Help
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Swing by Drop-In Tech Help at the Los Gatos Library on E Main St. Bring any device that's been bugging you, or just pop in and see what questions come to mind.
cards.why: Free tech help from real people is genuinely useful and hard to find.

cards.name: Elements Massage
cards.address: 1604 W Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Elements Massage in Campbell. It's a proper therapeutic session, not a rushed spa chain experience. Call ahead or check online for availability.
cards.why: A Tuesday afternoon massage is exactly as good as it sounds.

cards.name: Brew City Grill
cards.address: 651 W Hamilton Ave Suite 1070, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the evening at Brew City Grill in Campbell. Sit down, order something off the dinner menu, and take your time with it.
cards.why: A solid meal to cap a full day without overthinking it.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail with the kids — flat, easy, and the water keeps them entertained the whole way.
cards.why: Cool morning air is perfect for a low-key outdoor start before the day heats up.

cards.name: The Happy Hound of Los Gatos
cards.address: 15899 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Grab hot dogs and casual bites for the whole crew at this Los Gatos spot. Under $15 a head and the kids will be happy.
cards.why: Affordable, casual, and totally kid-friendly — hits the spot after the trail.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake for the lake loop, some grass time, and the little train the kids will absolutely want to ride.
cards.why: It's the classic Los Gatos family spot for a reason — there's something for every age here.

cards.name: Barnes & Noble
cards.address: 122 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the kids section at Barnes and Noble on Santa Cruz Ave — let them pick out a book or two and flip through things at the little tables.
cards.why: Great way to wind down the afternoon energy without screens.

cards.name: Crafty Corner (Ages 3-10)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Crafty Corner is a free drop-in art activity for kids ages 3 to 10 happening today at 110 E Main St. Let them make something and get their hands messy.
cards.why: Free, creative, and timed perfectly for the late afternoon lull.

cards.name: PintxoPote
cards.address: 424 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Grab pintxos and plates at PintxoPote on North Santa Cruz Ave. Order a spread of small bites and let the table graze.
cards.why: It's a fun, relaxed dinner format that works surprisingly well with kids.

cards.name: Manresa Bread
cards.address: 271 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Manresa Bread in Los Altos. Grab a pastry and a coffee, settle in, and take your time — this is the kind of bakery where you actually want to linger.
cards.why: Solid way to ease into a Tuesday morning with something worth getting out of bed for.

cards.name: High Meadow Vista Point
cards.address: High Meadow Trail, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to High Meadow Vista Point for a morning hike with good views over the hills. The trail is worth it, especially on a crisp morning like this.
cards.why: Cool temps make this way more enjoyable than a hot afternoon hike would be.

cards.name: American Cancer Society Discovery Shop - Los Altos, CA
cards.address: 243 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the American Cancer Society Discovery Shop on Main Street in Los Altos. It's a proper thrift browse — books, housewares, clothes, the occasional weird find.
cards.why: Great shop for a slow afternoon poke-around, and everything you spend goes somewhere good.

cards.name: Woodland Adult Book Discussion
cards.blurb: Join the Woodland Adult Book Discussion at the Woodland Branch Library. Come with opinions or just come to listen — either works.
cards.why: A Tuesday afternoon book discussion is a genuinely good use of a free afternoon.

cards.name: On Writing Action-Packed and Suspenseful Spycraft with Brad Taylor
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Head to 585 Franklin St for Brad Taylor's talk on writing action-packed spycraft fiction. Even if you've never read his books, it's a free author talk about a genuinely fun subject.
cards.why: Spy thriller craft talk on a Tuesday afternoon is a pretty unexpected treat.

cards.name: Beautiful, Easy-Care, Bearded Iris
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Cap the night with the Bearded Iris gardening talk back at 585 Franklin St. It's free, it's chill, and bearded irises are genuinely one of the easier and more rewarding plants to grow.
cards.why: A low-key evening event that wraps up the day on a nice, relaxed note.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the lake path. The kids can watch the birds, run along the open grass, and get some fresh air before the day fills up.
cards.why: Big open space, free, and the perfect way to shake off a cool morning before things warm up.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Head into the Computer History Museum for the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibit. It covers one of the wildest chapters in tech history and there's plenty of hands-on stuff around the museum to keep kids engaged too.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fascinating story and the museum is great for curious kids.

cards.name: Simply Sabor
cards.address: 340 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Simply Sabor on Castro Street. Casual Latin food, great flavors, and relaxed enough for a family stop in the middle of the day.
cards.why: One of the most loved spots on Castro and easy enough for everyone at the table.

cards.name: Read Me
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Pop back into the Computer History Museum for the Read Me exhibition. It explores the history and power of written language through interactive displays that kids actually find cool.
cards.why: You're already paying admission so might as well see another great exhibit while you're there.

cards.name: Linden Tree Children's Books
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Linden Tree Children's Books in Los Altos. It's the kind of bookstore where the kids will want to read everything on the shelf before you can drag them out. Let them pick something.
cards.why: Independent kids bookstores this good are rare and worth the trip.

cards.name: Cinemark Century Mountain View 16
cards.address: 1500 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Catch a movie at Cinemark Century Mountain View 16. Check what's showing on their app and grab popcorn. Good way to wrap a full day on a comfortable note.
cards.why: A movie is the perfect low-key ending when everyone's legs are done for the day.

cards.name: Academic Coffee
cards.address: 499 S Second St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Academic Coffee, a solid downtown cafe to wake up with an espresso drink and ease into the day.
cards.why: Good coffee and a chill spot to kick things off before heading into the rest of the day.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk the rose garden while it's still cool out. April is prime season and the blooms are genuinely worth it.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a morning stroll and it costs nothing.

cards.name: Black Maternal Health Week 2026: Rooted in Justice & Joy at King Library
cards.blurb: Catch the Black Maternal Health Week opening at King Library. It's a free exhibition rooted in community storytelling and art.
cards.why: Free, meaningful, and happening today — worth an hour of your morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch. Grab a banh mi, tacos, or ramen from whoever has the shorter line and find a patio seat.
cards.why: A dozen vendors under one roof makes it easy to eat exactly what you're in the mood for.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara for the free contemporary art collection and a wander through the sculpture garden out back.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely impressive work that most locals sleep on.

cards.name: Chew Cooking Classes at Student Wellness Center
cards.blurb: Check out the Chew Cooking Classes at the Student Wellness Center. It's a free hands-on cooking event worth dropping into.
cards.why: Free, fun, and a totally different vibe from the rest of the day.

cards.name: HealthLand Thai Spa
cards.address: 2937 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at HealthLand Thai Spa with a traditional Thai massage to close out the day properly.
cards.why: After a full day on your feet, this is exactly how you should end it.

cards.name: Almaden Lake Park
cards.address: 6099 Winfield Blvd, San Jose, CA 95120, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Almaden Lake Park for a morning walk around the lake. The kids can run the trails, feed ducks, and burn off energy before the day gets going.
cards.why: Free, wide open, and perfect for getting everyone moving before it warms up.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for lunch. Pick a different vendor each and meet back at a patio table. Tacos, banh mi, ramen — everyone gets what they want.
cards.why: No arguments about where to eat when there are a dozen options under one roof.

cards.name: Patient No More Exhibition at King Library
cards.blurb: Check out the Patient No More Exhibition at King Library. It's a thought-provoking free exhibit and a great way to introduce kids to social history in a real public space.
cards.why: Free admission and the library itself is a cool space for kids to explore.

cards.name: Chew Cooking Classes at Student Wellness Center
cards.blurb: Swing by the Chew Cooking Classes at the Student Wellness Center for a free hands-on cooking session. It's a fun, low-key activity the whole family can join.
cards.why: Free cooking classes are a great way to mix things up and get the kids involved in making something.

cards.name: The City Fish
cards.address: 30 E Santa Clara St #140, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at The City Fish for casual, affordable seafood downtown. Order at the counter, find a spot, and keep it easy for the end of the night.
cards.why: Under $15 a head and easy on everyone after a full day out.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit The Dish Trail up in the Stanford foothills. It's a real climb with sweeping views of the Bay once you get up top, and at 60°F it'll be perfect hiking weather.
cards.why: Cool temps and clear skies make this the best kind of morning hike.

cards.name: Seasons Noodles & Dumplings Garden
cards.address: 702 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Seasons Noodles and Dumplings Garden in Mountain View for a solid post-hike lunch. Get the dumplings.
cards.why: You'll be hungry after that trail and this hits exactly right.

cards.name: Living with OCD
cards.address: 866 Campus Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing by the Living with OCD exhibition on Stanford's campus. It's a thoughtful, well-put-together show that's free to walk through at your own pace.
cards.why: It's a genuinely interesting exhibit and a nice way to use the afternoon hours.

cards.name: Woodland Adult Book Discussion
cards.blurb: Join the Woodland Adult Book Discussion over in Los Altos. Good conversation, free, and no prep required if you just want to drop in and listen.
cards.why: It's a low-key Tuesday afternoon and this is exactly what Tuesday afternoons are for.

cards.name: His & Her Rocks Gallery
cards.address: 296 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to His and Her Rocks Gallery on State Street in Los Altos. It's a genuinely cool space full of minerals, crystals, and geological curiosities worth browsing slowly.
cards.why: You're already in Los Altos and this is one of those places that's way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: Roja Los Altos
cards.address: 242 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Roja Los Altos on State Street. It's a sit-down Mexican dinner worth the price, so order well and take your time.
cards.why: Great food to close out a full day, and you're already right there in Los Altos.

cards.name: Redwood Café by Chef Julien
cards.address: 450 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Redwood Café — grab a coffee for the grown-ups and something good to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a solid neighborhood spot that won't slow you down.

cards.name: Cooley Landing Park
cards.address: 2100 Bay Rd, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Let the kids run around and explore the waterfront at Cooley Landing Park — there's open space, bay views, and room to just wander.
cards.why: Fresh air and room to roam is a great way to start a Tuesday with kids.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center and make sure the kids find the giant Rodin sculptures outside — they're genuinely impressive and the kids can get up close.
cards.why: Free, world-class, and the outdoor sculpture garden keeps it from feeling like a stuffy museum visit.

cards.name: La Bodeguita del Medio
cards.address: 463 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to La Bodeguita del Medio for lunch. It's a fun, colorful spot on California Ave with good food and a casual vibe the whole family can settle into.
cards.why: The price is right and it's the kind of place where nobody has to be on their best behavior.

cards.name: LEGO Tuesdays!
cards.address: 270 Forest Ave. Palo Alto
cards.blurb: LEGO Tuesdays at 270 Forest Ave is exactly what it sounds like. Drop in, grab some bricks, and see what the kids build. Zero pressure, totally free.
cards.why: It's Tuesday, it's free, and kids absolutely light up at a table full of LEGOs.
